workflow.name: Escalate overdue and blocked Jira tickets with Gmail and Google Chat
workflow.description:
# How it works
- Fetches all open sprint tickets daily from your Jira project
- Analyzes each ticket for overdue days and blocked status
- Routes to the right escalation level: assignee email → team Google Chat alert → manager escalation

# Set up steps
- Add your Jira Software Cloud credentials in n8n
- Add your Gmail OAuth2 credentials in n8n
- Open the ⚙️ CONFIG node and fill in your 4 values (Jira domain, project key, manager email, Google Chat webhook)
- Test with the Manual Trigger, then enable the Schedule Trigger

parameters.content:
## LEVEL 1 -> REMINDER ASSIGNEE 🧠
- [0 - 2] Imminent DueDate → Send email to ticket assignee

## LEVEL 2 -> (WARNING ASSIGNEE OR NOTIFICATION CHANNEL) -> WARNING ⚠️

* **if [1 - 2] OverDue Days** → Send Email to ticket assignee

* **if ]2 - 4] OverDue Days** → Send notification to google chat dedicated channel

## LEVEL 3 -> ESCALATION MANAGER -> ALERT 🚨
* Greater than 4 OverDue Days → Send email to manager(s)

## Jira Tickets Monitoring — Deadlines & Blockages 🚨

Automate Jira ticket monitoring with progressive escalation to assignees, teams, and managers.


### How it works

Fetches all open sprint tickets daily and routes each one based on its status:

- 🧠 **Reminder** `[0–2 days left]` → Email to assignee
- ⚠️ **Warning** `[1–4 days overdue]` → Email to assignee or Google Chat alert
- 🚨 **Escalation** `[> 4 days overdue]` → Email to manager(s)
- 🔴 **Blocked** → Escalates over 3 days: assignee email → team chat → manager


### How to use

Edit only the **⚙️ CONFIG** node with your 4 values, then enable the Schedule Trigger.


### Requirements

- Jira Software Cloud
- Gmail (OAuth2)
- Google Chat Webhook


### ⚙️ CONFIG

- `JIRA_DOMAIN`: e.g `my-company.atlassian.net`
- `JIRA_PROJECT_KEY`: e.g. `PROJ`
- `MANAGER_EMAILS`: Escalation recipient(s)
- `GOOGLE_CHAT_WEBHOOK_URL`: Your Google Chat space webhook


### Need Help?

Visit 👉 [aixautomation.tech](https://aixautomation.tech/)

parameters.message: =Hello {{ $input.last().json.assignee.displayName }}, This is an alert for an overdue ticket: https://{{ $('⚙️ CONFIG').first().json.JIRA_DOMAIN }}/browse/{{ $input.last().json.key }}
parameters.subject: Jira DueDate Ticket Alert 🚨

parameters.content:
## Blocked Tickets (WARNING ASSIGNEE  ⚠️  OR ESCALATION EMAIL 🚨)
* Blocked + 1 Days → Send Email to ticket assignee
* Blocked + 2 Days -> Notify google chat team
* Blocked + 3 Days → send email to manager.

parameters.message: =Hello {{ $input.last().json.data.assignee.displayName }}, This an Alert for blocked ticket: https://{{ $('⚙️ CONFIG').first().json.JIRA_DOMAIN }}/browse/{{ $input.last().json.data.key }}
parameters.subject: Jira Blocked Ticket Alert 🚨

options.jql: =project = {{ $('⚙️ CONFIG').first().json.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }} AND status != Done AND sprint in openSprints()

parameters.content:
## TO CONSIDER FOR PRODUCTION: ⚠️ GMAIL / GOOGLE CHAT QUOTA ⚠️


### 💬 Google Chat Webhook Quotas
* **Per-Space Limit:** 60 messages / minute (Strict).
* **Per-Project Limit:** 3,000 messages / minute.
* **Max Message Size:** ~32 KB.
> **Tip:** In HTTP Node, set Batching to **1 item per 1000ms**.

---


### 📧 Gmail API Quotas (n8n Node)
* **Daily Limit (Workspace):** 2,000 recipients / day.
* **Daily Limit (Free):** 500 recipients / day.
* **Burst Rate:** ~1–3 emails per second.
> **Tip:** **Split In Batches** (Size 1) + **Wait Node** (1s).

---


### 🛠️ Error Handling Strategy
* **Retry on Failure:**
* **Max Retries:** 3
* **Delay Between Retries:** 5000ms (5 seconds).
* **Error Action:** "Continue" to avoid stopping the whole workflow.

nodes.name: NOTIFY_TEAM
nodes.notes:
Quota                       | TypeLimit (per 60 seconds)  |       Impact

Per-Project (Write)	 | 3,000 messages	| This is the total messages your "app" (webhook) can send across all spaces it has access to.

Per-Space (Write)	 | 60 messages	| This is the limit for a single chat space. If you send more than 1 message per second to one channel, you will hit this.

Message Size	         | ~32 KB	        |  A single message (including text and any card JSON) cannot exceed roughly 32 KB

parameters.message: =Hello {{ $input.last().json.data.assignee.displayName }}, This a warning for your blocked ticket: https://{{ $('⚙️ CONFIG').first().json.JIRA_DOMAIN }}/browse/{{ $input.last().json.data.key }}
parameters.subject: Jira Blocked Ticket Warning ⚠️

nodes.name: SEND_DUEDATE_REMINDER
nodes.notes:
Account                          |   TypeDaily Sending Limit   |    Burst Limit (Rate)

Free Gmail                      |  ~500 recipients/day          |    ~1 email per second

Google Workspace        |    ~2,000 recipients/day     |    ~2–3 emails per second

parameters.subject: Jira DueDate Ticket Reminder 🧠

parameters.subject: Jira DueDate Ticket  Warning ⚠️

nodes.notes:
Quota                       | TypeLimit (per 60 seconds)  |       Impact

Per-Project (Write)	 | 3,000 messages	| This is the total messages your "app" (webhook) can send across all spaces it has access to.

Per-Space (Write)	 | 60 messages	| This is the limit for a single chat space. If you send more than 1 message per second to one channel, you will hit this.

Message Size	         | ~32 KB	        |  A single message (including text and any card JSON) cannot exceed roughly 32 KB

assignments.name: MANAGER_EMAILS
assignments.value: user@example.com [ CAN BE LIST OF EMAILS ]

parameters.content:
## ⚙️ CONFIGURATION NODE
Set all your values here — **this is the only node you need to edit**

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
